root_text.an8.7:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate acirapakkante devadatte.
root_text.an8.7:1.2: Tatra bhagavā devadattaṁ ārabbha bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an8.7:1.3: “sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.7:1.4: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.7:1.5: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.7:1.6: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ parasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.7:1.7: Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.7:2.2: Lābhena hi, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.7:2.9: pāpamittatāya, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.7:2.10: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.7:3.1: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya,
root_text.an8.7:4.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya,
root_text.an8.7:5.1: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an8.7:5.8: uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an8.7:5.9: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya,

translation_text.an8.7:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain, not long after Devadatta had left.
translation_text.an8.7:1.2: There the Buddha spoke to the mendicants about Devadatta:
translation_text.an8.7:1.3: “Mendicants, it’s good for a mendicant to check their own failings from time to time.
translation_text.an8.7:1.4: It’s good for a mendicant to check the failings of others from time to time.
translation_text.an8.7:1.5: It’s good for a mendicant to check their own successes from time to time.
translation_text.an8.7:1.6: It’s good for a mendicant to check the successes of others from time to time.
translation_text.an8.7:1.7: Overcome and overwhelmed by eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.7:2.9: bad friendship, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.7:2.10: Overcome and overwhelmed by these eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.7:3.1: It’s good for a mendicant, whenever they encounter it, to overcome gain …
translation_text.an8.7:4.1: What advantage does a mendicant gain by overcoming these eight things?
translation_text.an8.7:5.1: The distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without overcoming these eight things do not arise when they have overcome them.
translation_text.an8.7:5.9: This is the advantage that a mendicant gains by overcoming these eight things.
translation_text.an8.7:6.1: So, mendicants, you should train like this:
translation_text.an8.7:6.2: ‘Whenever we encounter it, we will overcome gain …

variant_text.an8.7:5.1: uppannaṁ lābhaṁ anabhibhuyya → anabhibhuyya anabhibhuyya (mr) | lābhaṁ abhibhuyya → abhibhuyya abhibhuyya (mr)
